utility.name: JCP&L
utility.note: JCP&L (Jersey Central Power & Light) is the electric utility for almost all of Monmouth County. Its overhead distribution network runs through heavily wooded neighborhoods and along the Bayshore and coast, which is why a single nor'easter or summer thunderstorm can knock out power to whole towns and why restoration in the outer townships can stretch into multiple days.

stormContext: Monmouth County sits in the path of the nor'easters that batter the Jersey Shore every fall and winter, and these slow-moving storms are the single most common cause of multi-day power outages here.

Coastal and Bayshore communities — from the Navesink and Shrewsbury riverfronts to the bayfront sections of Middletown — are especially exposed to wind-driven outages and storm surge that can take down JCP&L lines.

Homeowners across the county remember Superstorm Sandy, when much of Monmouth lost power for a week or more. That experience is why a properly maintained standby generator is no longer a luxury here — it's how families keep heat, refrigeration, sump pumps, and well water running when the grid goes down.
